fact_nuggets.text: Insomnia (Japanese: Insomnia) is an Ability introduced in Generation III.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Prevents sleep. MD T D Protects the Pokemon from being put to sleep. MD S Protects the Pokemon from being made to sleep. B S L MD GtI Unavailable S MD The Pokemon won't get the sleep, napping, yawning, or nightmare status condition! MD RT DX The Pokemon won't get the Sleep, Napping, Yawning, or Nightmare condition.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation III 1.1.2 Generation IV 1.1.3 Generation V onward 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Insomnia prevents the Pokemon from being afflicted by sleep and Yawn . Rest will fail when used by the Pokemon. A Pokemon with this Ability will wake up if it is asleep in battle (such as if it gains the Ability outside of battle or by an effect like Skill Swap , or is put to sleep by a Pokemon with an Ability like Mold Breaker ). If the Pokemon with this Ability is asleep and is holding a Chesto or Lum Berry , Insomnia will cure the Pokemon of sleep before the Berry has a chance to be consumed. If a Pokemon with this Ability is drowsy due to Yawn, when it would fall asleep the Ability activates and keeps it awake. The move Worry Seed can change the target Pokemon's Ability to Insomnia. Generation III [ edit | edit source ] If a sleeping Pokemon with Insomnia is sent into battle as one of the lead Pokemon or to replace a fainted Pokemon, or gains Insomnia due to Trace , it will not be cured of sleep until after a Pokemon takes its turn (uses a move, switches out, etc.). It will be cured of sleep if it is switched in to replace a switched out Pokemon (including if switched in via Baton Pass ). If a sleeping Pokemon has Insomnia as its original Ability but not its current Ability, it will remain asleep when switched out. Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] When a sleeping Pokemon with Insomnia enters battle, it will be cured of sleep. If a sleeping Pokemon has Insomnia as its original Ability but not its current Ability, if it is one of the active Pokemon at the end of the battle or switched out, it will be cured of sleep (with no message). Generation V onward [ edit | edit source ] If a sleeping Pokemon has Insomnia as its original Ability but not its current Ability, it will remain asleep when switched out. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] If a sleeping Pokemon gains Insomnia by Evolution or the Ability Capsule , it will not wake up until sent into battle. Pokemon with Insomnia will not be put to sleep by the Gentleman 's Kirlia in the Battle Pike nor the Battle Arcade 's game board.
